creator: demik+freebsd@lostwave.net
text:
We worked somewhat on this on #freebsd-fr (thanks to olivier who found the root cause, at least we think so)

The issue lies in libgcrypt build system. That build system has a -O flag Munging option which is enabled on FreeBSD

Should have something like this in the build logs:
checking whether a -O flag munging is requested... yes

This is supposed to sanitise -O3s except that doesn't work:
The build stack is leading with optimisations such as -O2 and -O0 where needed. However it adds CFLAGS or CXXFLAGS after that, which gives stuff like:

libtool: compile:  cc -DHAVE_CONFIG_H -I. -I.. -I../src -I../src -I/usr/local/include -O0 -pipe -O3
libtool: compile:  cc -DHAVE_CONFIG_H -I. -I.. -I../src -I../src -I/usr/local/include -O2 -pipe -O3

In two Makefile.am files, the munging thing is trying to remove -O3s:
o_flag_munging = sed -e 's/[[:blank:]]-O\([2-9sgz][2-9sgz]*\)/ -O1 /' -e 's/[[:blank:]]-Ofast/ -O1 /g'

Except it doesn't, because it's only looking for the first -O flag… (missing /g on the first regexp)

The good news is that nobody else noticed that as well and fixed it upstream:
https://github.com/gpg/libgcrypt/commit/e96df0c82e086bf348753d2d0fa37fa6191b4b14

So we will just have to wait for the next release I guess…
